topRecommendations: Provide clear and distinct labels for each card to facilitate easy navigation and understanding of plan differences.
Implement ARIA attributes or other accessibility features to ensure screen readers can effectively convey the structure and purpose of each card and button.
Emphasize the primary call-to-action for each plan to reduce friction
Use clear and concise headings for each section of the cards to improve clarity
Consider using a more prominent button variant for the plan selection to guide the user's attention
Provide an explicit and prominent call-to-action for the free plan
Consider separate and clear actions for each plan to reduce confusion
Use more detailed and explicit helper text to clarify the implications of each choice
topFrictionPoints: The grid layout of cards may cause navigation issues for screen-reader-reliant users without clear grouping or separator elements.
Similar button labels (e.g., 'Go to Builder' and 'Subscribe') across different plans may cause confusion.
The 'Go to Builder' button on the Free plan may cause confusion as it does not directly relate to the plan selection task
The buttons on the cards have a 'secondary' variant which may not be prominent enough for a busy mobile-first user
The dense list of features in the CardBody may cause cognitive load for Sam Rushed
No clear call-to-action for first-time users to start with the free plan
Similar button labels for different plans (Go to Builder vs Subscribe)
no clear call to action on pricing page
topConfusionPoints: The meaning of 'Go to Builder' vs 'Subscribe' can be inferred but may not be immediately clear without additional context or visual cues.
The difference between the plans is not immediately clear due to the amount of information presented
The CardHeader and CardBody do not have clear headings to differentiate them from the rest of the content
The difference between free and paid plans is somewhat detailed but could be more explicit for cautious users
No explicit statement about what happens after clicking 'Subscribe' or 'Go to Builder'
no indication of potential add-on costs or fees
The 'Free' plan's features are not clearly distinguished from the paid plans
The 'UI patterns & guidelines' and 'per-component guidelines' features may require additional explanation for clarity
